arts.ttl: Pep Guardiola claims he would QUIT football before following in Jurgen Klopp's footsteps and becoming an executive - as manager insists he still 'loves' coaching amid uncertainty over Man City future
arts.sum: JOE BERNSTEIN: Guardiola is adamant he still loves the day-to-day involvement at Manchester City even as the club wait to hear if he wants to extend his current contract.

arts.ttl: Man City willing to sell Walker - Sunday's gossip
arts.sum: Manchester City might sell Kyle Walker next summer, Liverpool eye a Virgil van Dijk replacement should he leave Anfield, plus more.

arts.ttl: I’m an emotional man: Pep Guardiola embarrassed by his touchline reactions
arts.sum: Manchester City’s inspirational boss can sometimes cut a frenetic figure in the technical area.

arts.ttl: Gary O'Neil points the finger of blame for Wolves struggles with strong vow to critics
arts.sum: Wolves are yet to taste victory in the Premier League this season - and have just one point from their opening seven matches - with Manchester City heading to Molineux this weekend

arts.ttl: Jack Grealish reveals what sets Man City apart in Premier League title race
arts.sum: Manchester City are battling to win their fifth Premier League title in succession this season and Jack Grealish has sent a strong warning to their rivals ahead of Sunday's clash with Wolves

arts.ttl: Pep Guardiola makes admission on Man City future with contract set to expire next summer
arts.sum: Pep Guardiola's current deal at Manchester City is up at the end of the season - but the Catalan insists he is still enjoying his work with the Blues

arts.ttl: Pep Guardiola pays ultimate Sir Alex Ferguson compliment after Man Utd axe
arts.sum: Manchester United icon Sir Alex Ferguson has been let go from his £2million-a-year ambassadorial contract at Old Trafford and Pep Guardiola has come out in support

arts.ttl: Pep Guardiola pledges to quit football when he loses love of the game
arts.sum: Manchester City manager ‘never expected’ huge success City visit struggling Wolves on Sunday in Premier League Pep Guardiola says he will quit football entirely when he loses the passion to manage after revealing he has no ambition to take a different role in the game. The Manchester City manager’s contract ends next summer but he is yet to decide whether to stay, although he would consider managing elsewhere. City travel to Molineux, where they suffered their first league defeat of last season, on Sunday hoping to maintain their unbeaten start, having won five of their opening seven matches. Guardiola is chasing a fifth consecutive Premier League title and the seventh of his nine-year tenure. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Kylian Mbappé overtakes Erling Haaland with latest Real Madrid goal
arts.sum: Real Madrid star Kylian Mbappé on Saturday took sole ownership of the title of top-scorer in Europe’s top-5 leagues this calendar year. As much came courtesy of his exploits in…

arts.ttl: ‘Unbelievable’: Jack Grealish says Liverpool have an incredible player who’s judged way too harshly
arts.sum: Trent Alexander-Arnold is undoubtedly one of the best players in the world. Alexander-Arnold is a target for Real Madrid for a reason, he’s a world-class...
